title: Bridge Essay: Making sense: Dopamine activates conscious self-monitoring through medial prefrontal cortex.
concept: Metacognition as self-observation — neural substrate of knowing that one knows.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.
**Phase context:** Metacognition as self-observation — neural substrate of knowing that one knows.

**Making sense: Dopamine activates conscious self-monitoring through medial prefrontal cortex.** — Hum Brain Mapp (2015). DOI: 10.1002/hbm.22742. PMID: 25627861. When experiences become meaningful to the self, they are linked to synchronous activity in a paralimbic network of self-awareness and dopaminergic activity. This network includes medial prefrontal and medial parietal/posterior cingulate cortices, where transcranial magnetic stimulation may transiently impair self-awareness. Conversely, we hypothesize that dopaminergic stimulation may improve self-awareness and metacognition (i.e., the ability of the brain to consciously monitor its own cognitive
